examples.title: Wall-Mount Shelf Bracket
examples.subtitle: Cantilever with gravity load
examples.image_alt: L-shaped bracket with back plate highlighted green (fixed) and top shelf surface highlighted red with downward arrows (force).
examples.scenario: L-shaped bracket screwed to a wall, supporting a shelf. Classic cantilever bending. The highest stress occurs at the inner corner of the L-bend.

supports.description: Back plate (the face against the wall, where screws go).
supports.instructions: Click the flat back face. It should highlight entirely.

forces.estimation: Shelf load 5 kg x 10 N/kg = 50 N

key_insights: Highest stress at the inner corner of the L-bend.
If the bracket fails, it will fail at the bend.
Increase infill density in the bend region.
examples.expected_stress: Stress concentration at the inner corner of the L-bend. Low stress on the flat plates.

examples.title: Robot Arm Joint Bracket
examples.subtitle: Fixed + Force + Torque
examples.image_alt: Structural bracket with upper plate highlighted green (fixed), lower bore highlighted red (force down), and flange face highlighted blue with curved arrows (torque).
examples.scenario: Structural bracket connecting a servo gearbox to a robot arm base. Handles vertical load (arm weight) and rotational load (servo torque).

supports.description: Upper flat plate (bolted to base frame).
supports.instructions: Click the top flat mounting face.

forces.description: Arm weight + payload acting on gearbox seat.
forces.estimation: Arm 0.5 kg + payload 4.5 kg = 5 kg x 10 = 50 N

torques.description: Servo output torque through flange bolt circle.
torques.face: Circular flange face (NOT the hole inner wall)

key_insights: Select the flange FACE for torque, not the hole wall.
Torque transfers through the bolt pattern on the flat face.
Highest stress at the bracket's narrow neck.
examples.expected_stress: Highest stress at the bracket's narrow neck between mounting plate and gearbox seat. Moderate stress around bolt holes.

examples.title: Motor Mount Housing
examples.subtitle: Vibration + bolt holes
examples.image_alt: Box-shaped housing with bottom feet highlighted green (fixed), motor seat highlighted red (force), and motor ring highlighted blue with curved arrows (torque).
examples.scenario: Enclosure for a small DC motor or gearbox. Must resist vibration forces and torque reaction from the motor.

supports.instructions: Use Fix Bolt Holes quick setup, or Surface mode on the bottom plate.

forces.description: Motor vibration force on motor seat.

key_insights: Vibration forces are cyclic - use higher safety factor (3.0x).
Linear FEA cannot model fatigue directly.
Higher safety factor provides fatigue margin.
examples.expected_stress: Stress around motor seat and bolt holes. Low stress on housing walls (unless thin).

examples.title: Structural Beam
examples.subtitle: Simply supported with distributed load
examples.image_alt: Horizontal beam with both end faces highlighted green (fixed) and top face highlighted red with downward arrows (force).
examples.scenario: Horizontal beam supported at both ends, loaded from above. Common for shelving crossbars and bridging elements.

supports.instructions: Click each end face using Surface mode.

forces.estimation: Total weight the beam carries (e.g., 10 kg = 100 N)

key_insights: Max stress at the center (bottom in tension, top in compression).
Classic bending pattern visible in stress map.
Supports on both ends prevent cantilever behavior.
examples.expected_stress: Maximum stress at mid-span, decreasing toward supports. Bottom face in tension (higher stress).

examples.title: Clamped Plate with Pressure
examples.subtitle: Edges fixed, face loaded
examples.image_alt: Flat plate with all four edge faces highlighted green (fixed) and the large top face highlighted red with downward arrows (pressure).
examples.scenario: Flat plate clamped around its edges under uniform pressure. Examples: enclosure lid, window panel, tank wall.

supports.description: All four edge faces (or entire perimeter).
supports.instructions: Select each edge face using Surface mode.

key_insights: If deflection > 10% of thickness, linear assumption is less accurate.
Use Fine mesh for thin plates to capture bending gradient.
Max stress at center and clamped edges.
examples.expected_stress: Maximum stress at the center and at the clamped edges. Zero stress at mid-thickness.

examples.title: Bolt-On Handle / Lever
examples.subtitle: Grip force + bolt hole mounting
examples.image_alt: Handle shape with bolt holes at one end highlighted green (fixed) and grip area at the other end highlighted red with arrows (force).
examples.scenario: Handle or lever bolted at one end, gripped at the other. Examples: cabinet handle, tool lever, crank arm.

supports.description: Bolt holes at the attachment end.
supports.instructions: Use Fix Bolt Holes quick setup, or Hole mode on each bolt hole.

forces.description: Grip force at the free end.
forces.face: Grip area at the free end

key_insights: Highest stress at the base near bolt holes, not at the grip.
Design the thickest section at the mounting flange.
Ensure bolt hole diameter matches your actual screws.
examples.expected_stress: Stress concentrated at the junction between handle shaft and mounting flange. Secondary stress around bolt holes.
